texts.text: 4th Floor, Block III, Delhi IT Park, Shastri Park New Delhi -110053

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Parichay Authenticator is a robust authentication framework that offers a variety of multifactor authentication solutions like Token Authentication and Tap Authentication to provide the access to Parichay, Jan Parichay, and others popular services. It also helps in securing the device by providing local device authentication parameters which includes Pattern, Pin, and Fingerprint.
texts.text: Parichay Authenticator is a robust authentication framework that offers a variety of multifactor authentication solutions like Token Authentication and Tap Authentication to provide the access to Parichay, Jan Parichay, and others popular services. It also helps in securing the device by providing local device authentication parameters which includes Pattern, Pin, and Fingerprint.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  application  authenticates  users  via  a  two-step  multi-factor  authentication process where users need to provide a secondary factor of authentication along with the password.
texts.text: The  application  authenticates  users  via  a  two-step  multi-factor  authentication process where users need to provide a secondary factor of authentication along with the password.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For  Tap-based  authentication,  users  are  requested  to  tap  a  prompt  sent  by  the authenticator on registered client device. The prompt will ask if the user wants to ' accept ' or ' deny ' the access request for an online application. Following are the benefits of Tap Authentication:
texts.text: For  Tap-based  authentication,  users  are  requested  to  tap  a  prompt  sent  by  the authenticator on registered client device. The prompt will ask if the user wants to ' accept ' or ' deny ' the access request for an online application. Following are the benefits of Tap Authentication:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Easy, quick and convenient real-time authentication solution.
texts.text: Easy, quick and convenient real-time authentication solution.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Authenticates user identity with a single tap.
texts.text: Authenticates user identity with a single tap.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Single-tap authentication effectively helps in securing the data in a hassle-free way in addition to meeting the required compliances.
texts.text: Single-tap authentication effectively helps in securing the data in a hassle-free way in addition to meeting the required compliances.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Access Parichay/JanParichay and integrated services with a single tap.
texts.text: Access Parichay/JanParichay and integrated services with a single tap.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For  Token-based  authentication,  users  are  requested  to  provide  a  soft  token generated  every  30  seconds  by  the  Authenticator  web  and  installed  application simultaneously. Following are the benefits of Token Authentication.
texts.text: For  Token-based  authentication,  users  are  requested  to  provide  a  soft  token generated  every  30  seconds  by  the  Authenticator  web  and  installed  application simultaneously. Following are the benefits of Token Authentication.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · This  authentication  factor  adds  a  second  layer  of  security  to  application, network, or service access.
texts.text: This  authentication  factor  adds  a  second  layer  of  security  to  application, network, or service access.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Provides seamless access to Jan Parichay and integrated services.
texts.text: Provides seamless access to Jan Parichay and integrated services.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Helps users access the service during the offline mode.
texts.text: Helps users access the service during the offline mode.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Download the Parichay Authenticator Application on your smartphone from Google Play Store or use the icon links available on Parichay Home Page to download the application APK. (Refer fig. 1)
texts.text: Download the Parichay Authenticator Application on your smartphone from Google Play Store or use the icon links available on Parichay Home Page to download the application APK. (Refer fig. 1)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Steps to enable tap/token authentication on Parichay Authenticator
texts.text: Steps to enable tap/token authentication on Parichay Authenticator

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users can enable MFA by logging in to their Parichay Account and following the steps givenbelow afterwards:
texts.text: Users can enable MFA by logging in to their Parichay Account and following the steps givenbelow afterwards:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Step 1: Click on the top-right ' button ' to see the ' Settings ' option. [Refer fig. 2]
texts.text: Step 1: Click on the top-right ' button ' to see the ' Settings ' option. [Refer fig. 2]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 2 : Click on the ' Settings ' option to proceed. [Refer fig. 3]
texts.text: Step 2 : Click on the ' Settings ' option to proceed. [Refer fig. 3]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 7 : Click to switch the button from 'OFF' to 'ON'' and enable the Multi-factor Authentication in Account Settings tab. [Refer fig. 4]
texts.text: Step 7 : Click to switch the button from 'OFF' to 'ON'' and enable the Multi-factor Authentication in Account Settings tab. [Refer fig. 4]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 8: Now, scan the QR using the Scanner feature of Parichay Authenticator app on your mobile. [Refer fig. 5]
texts.text: Step 8: Now, scan the QR using the Scanner feature of Parichay Authenticator app on your mobile. [Refer fig. 5]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 9 : Open the Parichay Authenticator application on your smartphone (Android/iOS). Slide forward or click on the 'Skip/Proceed' button. [Refer fig. 6]
texts.text: Step 9 : Open the Parichay Authenticator application on your smartphone (Android/iOS). Slide forward or click on the 'Skip/Proceed' button. [Refer fig. 6]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 10: Click to choose the 'Screen lock type' or 'Skip' to continue. [Refer fig. ]
texts.text: Step 10: Click to choose the 'Screen lock type' or 'Skip' to continue. [Refer fig. ]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 11.1.1: Click on the 'Scan QR Code' Button. [Refer fig. ]
texts.text: Step 11.1.1: Click on the 'Scan QR Code' Button. [Refer fig. ]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step  11.1.2: Scan  the  QR  [Refer  fig.  11]  using  the  scan  feature  of  Parichay  Authenticator application. [Refer fig. 9]
texts.text: Step  11.1.2: Scan  the  QR  [Refer  fig.  11]  using  the  scan  feature  of  Parichay  Authenticator application. [Refer fig. 9]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Now, the user will be able to see the token on Parichay Authenticator, use this to configure your account. [Refer fig. 10]
texts.text: Now, the user will be able to see the token on Parichay Authenticator, use this to configure your account. [Refer fig. 10]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 11.1.3: Enter the six-digit token number in the required field below the QR Code. [Refer Fig. 17]
texts.text: Step 11.1.3: Enter the six-digit token number in the required field below the QR Code. [Refer Fig. 17]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Now, the user has successfully configured their account with Parichay Authenticator for both tap and token authentication.
texts.text: Now, the user has successfully configured their account with Parichay Authenticator for both tap and token authentication.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The user can configure their account using the Auth Key method by following the steps given below:
texts.text: The user can configure their account using the Auth Key method by following the steps given below:

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 11.2.1: Click on the 'Enter Auth Key' Button. [Refer fig. 12]
texts.text: Step 11.2.1: Click on the 'Enter Auth Key' Button. [Refer fig. 12]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 11.2.2: Now, Enter the 'User ID' and 'Auth Key' mentioned on the web screen in the required fields. [Refer fig. 13]
texts.text: Step 11.2.2: Now, Enter the 'User ID' and 'Auth Key' mentioned on the web screen in the required fields. [Refer fig. 13]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 11.2.3: Now, tap the drop-down to choose the 'Service Name' to continue. [Refer fig. 14]
texts.text: Step 11.2.3: Now, tap the drop-down to choose the 'Service Name' to continue. [Refer fig. 14]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 11.2.4: Click the 'Confirm' button to continue. [Refer fig. 15]
texts.text: Step 11.2.4: Click the 'Confirm' button to continue. [Refer fig. 15]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 11.2.5: Now, copy the token from Mobile Application and paste it on the required field given on the web screen [Refer fig. 16]
texts.text: Step 11.2.5: Now, copy the token from Mobile Application and paste it on the required field given on the web screen [Refer fig. 16]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Now, the user has successfully configured their account with Parichay Authenticator for both tap and token authentication. [Refer fig. 17]
texts.text: Now, the user has successfully configured their account with Parichay Authenticator for both tap and token authentication. [Refer fig. 17]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 1: User can choose either password-based or password-less authentication in the first step of login process:
texts.text: Step 1: User can choose either password-based or password-less authentication in the first step of login process:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: >> The user will be prompted to MFA page.
texts.text: >> The user will be prompted to MFA page.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Step 2: Now, choose any of the MFA options to continue. [Refer fig. 20]
texts.text: Step 2: Now, choose any of the MFA options to continue. [Refer fig. 20]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: >> User can choose any of the MFA options and following will be the authentication scenarios:
texts.text: >> User can choose any of the MFA options and following will be the authentication scenarios:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Step 3.1: OTP as SMS and on Sandes
texts.text: Step 3.1: OTP as SMS and on Sandes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Enter the 6-digit OTP received on the registered mobile no. and click Next to login. [Refer Fig. 21]
texts.text: Enter the 6-digit OTP received on the registered mobile no. and click Next to login. [Refer Fig. 21]

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: >> Post-successful verification, user will be redirected to Parichay Dashboard.
texts.text: >> Post-successful verification, user will be redirected to Parichay Dashboard.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a. User needs to choose the ' Tap Authentication ' and click ' Next ' to proceed. [Refer Fig. 22]
texts.text: User needs to choose the ' Tap Authentication ' and click ' Next ' to proceed. [Refer Fig. 22]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Now, User needs to check the pull notification for their Parichay Authenticator App.
texts.text: Now, User needs to check the pull notification for their Parichay Authenticator App.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: b. Tap the 'Accept' option to continue. [Refer Fig. 23]
texts.text: b. Tap the 'Accept' option to continue. [Refer Fig. 23]

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: >> Post-successful verification, user will be redirected to Parichay Dashboard.
texts.text: >> Post-successful verification, user will be redirected to Parichay Dashboard.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a. Choose the ' Token Authentication ' and click ' Next ' to proceed. [Refer Fig. 24]
texts.text: Choose the ' Token Authentication ' and click ' Next ' to proceed. [Refer Fig. 24]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Now, User needs to check the 6-digit 'Token' for their Parichay Authenticator App.
texts.text: Now, User needs to check the 6-digit 'Token' for their Parichay Authenticator App.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b. Enter the 'Token' in the required field to continue. [Refer Fig. 25]
texts.text: Enter the 'Token' in the required field to continue. [Refer Fig. 25]

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: >> Post-successful verification, user will be redirected to Parichay Dashboard.
texts.text: >> Post-successful verification, user will be redirected to Parichay Dashboard.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: User can manage geofencing for their Parichay Account accessibility via Parichay Authenticator Application. Following is the step-wise procedure:
texts.text: User can manage geofencing for their Parichay Account accessibility via Parichay Authenticator Application. Following is the step-wise procedure:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Step 1 : Open the Parichay Authenticator application configured with Parichay Account and Click the ' Geofencing ' icon to proceed. [Refer Fig. 26]
texts.text: Step 1 : Open the Parichay Authenticator application configured with Parichay Account and Click the ' Geofencing ' icon to proceed. [Refer Fig. 26]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 2: Click to switch the button and enable Geofencing Settings. [Refer Fig. 27]
texts.text: Step 2: Click to switch the button and enable Geofencing Settings. [Refer Fig. 27]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 3: Now, click the 'Manage' button to view the list of countries. [Refer Fig. 28]
texts.text: Step 3: Now, click the 'Manage' button to view the list of countries. [Refer Fig. 28]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 4: Mark/unmark to choose the countries as per your requirements. [Refer Fig. 29]
texts.text: Step 4: Mark/unmark to choose the countries as per your requirements. [Refer Fig. 29]

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Step 5: Now, click the ' Submit' button and press back to view the applied changes. [Refer Fig. 30]
texts.text: Step 5: Now, click the ' Submit' button and press back to view the applied changes. [Refer Fig. 30]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Parichay Authenticator provides user with an additional functionality of using Pull Notification to view the notification (if not received directly) from the application. The option is required when there is a weak network connectivity or has higher latency in firebase notification. [Refer Fig. 29]
texts.text: Parichay Authenticator provides user with an additional functionality of using Pull Notification to view the notification (if not received directly) from the application. The option is required when there is a weak network connectivity or has higher latency in firebase notification. [Refer Fig. 29]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users can edit their username by 'pressing' the account for 2 seconds. The feature provides users with the flexibility to use custom user names on service cards. [Refer Fig. 30]
texts.text: Users can edit their username by 'pressing' the account for 2 seconds. The feature provides users with the flexibility to use custom user names on service cards. [Refer Fig. 30]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The button ' what's new ' provided at the top-left corner of the screen, enables the users to access the list and details of latest functionalities related to the Authenticator application. [Refer Fig. 31]
texts.text: The button ' what's new ' provided at the top-left corner of the screen, enables the users to access the list and details of latest functionalities related to the Authenticator application. [Refer Fig. 31]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: One can click the top-left menu to see and choose the user support options such as: FAQs, About Us, and Change Screen lock. (Refer fig. 32)
texts.text: One can click the top-left menu to see and choose the user support options such as: FAQs, About Us, and Change Screen lock. (Refer fig. 32)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Multifactor  authentication  is  a  security  system  that  utilizes  more  than  one  method  of  user identification for a login or other transaction. This provides an additional layer of security over traditional forms of single sign-on authentication, which require only one type of identification such as a password.
texts.text: Multifactor  authentication  is  a  security  system  that  utilizes  more  than  one  method  of  user identification for a login or other transaction. This provides an additional layer of security over traditional forms of single sign-on authentication, which require only one type of identification such as a password.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note: The Token will start blinking RED in color to aware user about the last 5 seconds of token validity, after that the Token number will be updated.
texts.text: Note: The Token will start blinking RED in color to aware user about the last 5 seconds of token validity, after that the Token number will be updated.

table_cells.text: Steps to enable tap/token authentication on Parichay Authenticator ___________________

grid.text: Steps to enable tap/token authentication on Parichay Authenticator ___________________
Steps to enable tap/token authentication on Parichay Authenticator ___________________

table_cells.text: A one-time dynamic password that is valid only for 10 min
The web application sends OTP to the registered mobile no./email Id
The web application sends a tap notification to the mobile app with 'Accept' and 'Deny' options. On clicking Accept, the user will login to Parichay/Jan Parichay-Meri Pehchaan
The access is denied either on clicking Denied or after 60 seconds whichever is earlier. After denial, the user is redirected to the login page.
Parichay Authenticators app simultaneously generates a token every 30 seconds.
Even if the device/mobile has no access to the network, the user can enter this token to log into the Jan Parichay.

grid.text: A one-time dynamic password that is valid only for 10 min
The web application sends OTP to the registered mobile no./email Id
The web application sends a tap notification to the mobile app with 'Accept' and 'Deny' options. On clicking Accept, the user will login to Parichay/Jan Parichay-Meri Pehchaan
The access is denied either on clicking Denied or after 60 seconds whichever is earlier. After denial, the user is redirected to the login page.
Parichay Authenticators app simultaneously generates a token every 30 seconds.
Even if the device/mobile has no access to the network, the user can enter this token to log into the Jan Parichay.
